_note: SYNTHETIC DEMO FIXTURE — not investment advice, not real CAVA disclosures. The 'Meridian Securities' note is fictional; 10-K-style facts are approximate and illustrative. This is the grounding corpus the copilot retrieves from Moss mid-call: the fund's own research note + the company's filing. The follow-up grounding (Q2) and the contradiction flag (Q4) both point back to chunks in here. In production this is Unsiloed-parsed into the Moss `knowledge` index; here it is the source of truth for what 'their published model says.'

chunks.text: New-unit economics. We model FY26E new-unit AUV of $2.6M, with year-one new-unit restaurant-level margin of ~20%, ramping toward the fleet ~25% by year two. Cash-on-cash returns of >35% on a ~$1.2M net build cost anchor our long-term unit-growth algorithm. The premium to fast-casual peers is the crux of the bull case; we need recent vintages to keep opening at or near this AUV.

Comp build. Our +8% FY26E same-restaurant-sales estimate is TRAFFIC-LED: approximately +5% traffic and +3% effective price/mix. We view CAVA's comp quality as traffic-driven, which distinguishes it from price-reliant fast-casual peers and supports a premium multiple. A shift toward price-led comp would, in our view, lower comp quality and the durability of the runway.

Unit growth. We model ~17% FY26E unit growth (~65 net new restaurants), which management has indicated is fully covered by the signed-lease pipeline, with site selection extending into new inland markets beyond the coastal core.

Fleet metrics. As of fiscal year-end the company operated approximately 367 CAVA restaurants, with average unit volume (AUV) of approximately $2.9M and restaurant-level profit margin of approximately 25%. The company targets approximately 1,000 CAVA restaurants by 2032. New proteins, including grilled steak, were introduced to broaden the menu and lift average check.

Digital and loyalty. Digital channels represent approximately 35% of revenue. The company relaunched its loyalty program with a more flexible, points-based structure intended to increase visit frequency and personalize offers, replacing the prior visit-threshold rewards model.

Category and competition. The company positions Mediterranean as the next major fast-casual category, citing health-forward, customizable, broadly appealing cuisine. It competes with other fast-casual concepts for guests and real estate, and historically converted a number of acquired Zoes Kitchen locations into CAVA restaurants.
